title: Void Basement
shortDescription: An infinite basement where visitors slowly lose their senses and dissolve, piece by piece, into nothingness.
atmosphere: A pitch-black void of growing disorientation and despair as explorers lose form and, eventually, consciousness.
tips: Avoid basements reached from Level 4 — this level induces an irreversible fade from reality.

title: The Pit
shortDescription: A deep cavern reached by falling through pitfalls: tight passages, an algae bog and crushingly narrow squeezes with few ways out.
atmosphere: Oppressive, flooded caves reeking of salt and decay; unstable walls and crushing dark broken by bioluminescent fungi.
tips: Keep a hand on the wall through the maze. Crouch during tremors. Never touch bog walls or kick creatures. Angle carefully through the squeeze.

title: The Redlands
shortDescription: A crimson island drowned in darkness and swarmed by entities; only the Silver Castle, under CORAN's protection, offers refuge.
atmosphere: Perpetual darkness under a full moon, suffocating black clouds and endless hostile creatures ringing a gothic sanctuary.
tips: Sprint straight for the Silver Castle — don't look around or slow down. Bang on the doors for entry. Never use artificial light.

title: Stage Fright
shortDescription: A theatre stage of looping hallways populated by dream-like figures resembling people the visitor knows.
atmosphere: Theatrical and familiar at first, growing more unsettling as isolation deepens and hallucinations intensify.
tips: Leave through the trapdoors within two hours — longer stays badly erode your grip on reality.

title: Liminality
shortDescription: Sandy corridors of peeling painted walls, sun-like light and endless rain sounds, scattered with children's toys and old footprints.
atmosphere: An eerie yet soothing emptiness that evokes childhood memories despite the cracked paint and abandoned rooms.
tips: Mostly accessible to younger travelers — adults usually need an accompanying child. Windows and doorways make easy exits.

title: Back from the Charwood Brink
shortDescription: A subtropical highland of tea plantations and mountains, dotted with mysterious Saola statues, still healing from an ancient fire.
atmosphere: Humid, lush terrain thick with tea-leaf scent; an unusual 21-hour daylight cycle, peaceful but tinged with mystery.
tips: Bring a light for the 3-hour dark stretch. Navigate by the hut waypoints. Never try to start a fire. Trade with Smoky Steep Farm.

title: Oh, Shenandoah
shortDescription: Foggy pastoral hills lit by endless daylight, ringed by a permanently dark forest of massive insectoid colonies and odd sheep herds.
atmosphere: Persistent fog over rolling grassland; unchanging daylight in the hills against the perpetually dark deciduous border forest.
tips: Avoid the forest border where the insectoids thrive. Move carefully through the fogged center. The sheep are docile and often mark safe paths.

title: Space Station
shortDescription: A vast weightless space station stretching endlessly in every direction, full of dead machinery, overgrown gardens and starry portholes.
atmosphere: Desolate, disorienting null-gravity halls of dead equipment and plants; the shifting stars through the portholes hypnotize.
tips: Don't stare too long at the portholes — the pull toward the airlocks is real. Avoid lavatories where Clumps hide. Find a working spacesuit before you try to leave.

title: Ghosts in the Machine
shortDescription: An abandoned mid-20th-century medical research facility racked by time anomalies, bearing traces of unethical experiments on children.
atmosphere: A decrepit clinic of ominous machinery where temporal distortion makes you feel like you're phasing through reality itself.
tips: Don't linger — the entrance relocates unpredictably, and a 16-clock machine triggers spatial-displacement tremors every 30 minutes.

title: Sententia Oblita
shortDescription: A sinking swamp of incomplete structures and forgotten beings, its architectural fragments slowly swallowed by murky water.
atmosphere: An eerie, desolate bog where uncanny, unfinished entities feel like abandoned concepts or forgotten stories.
tips: Seek high ground and the rusted staircase set in the giant cube wall. The more coherent inhabitants may point you toward an exit.
